_meta.reference_population: AbRef-458 clinical/engineered antibody cohort + CDR physicochemical thresholds
_meta.client_reporting: Show professional recommendations only; do not expose raw structural thresholds or internal scoring trees.

hard_protected: residues within 5 A of CDR loops when structure is available
residues within 5 A of the VH/VL interface when structure is available
canonical disulfide cysteines and conserved scaffold anchors
preferred_candidates: surface-exposed framework charged sites needed for pI or patch balancing
framework instability motifs outside protected structural neighborhoods

exposed_hydrophobic_patch.intent: Reduce aggregation, hydrophobic patch, HIC/SAP-like burden
exposed_hydrophobic_patch.substitution_style: conservative hydrophobic-to-polar or less oxidation-sensitive substitution
exposed_hydrophobic_patch.immunogenicity_note: Generally favorable or neutral; still avoid creating rare sequence contexts.

exposed_charged_hydrophilic_surface.intent: Tune pI, net charge, PPC/PNC only when metric gates require action
exposed_charged_hydrophilic_surface.substitution_style: germline-compatible neutralization or conservative same-class replacement
exposed_charged_hydrophilic_surface.immunogenicity_note: Requires immunogenicity review when multiple charged/polar changes are introduced.

buried_hydrophobic_core.intent: Preserve antibody scaffold, folding, and VH/VL packing
buried_hydrophobic_core.immunogenicity_note: Developability gain does not justify scaffold destabilization risk.

cdr_policy.rationale: Clinical antibodies can retain CDR liabilities because these residues may be antigen-binding hotspots.
cdr_policy.allowed_report_language: CDR finding is advisory; modifications require structure and functional validation.
cdr_policy.forbidden_report_language: Do not provide automatic CDR substitution lists in CMC reports.
